events.text:
>
```json
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 50,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.85,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Belt mistracking due to off-center loading and skewed idlers creates pinch and nip points along the conveyor path.",
    "Skewed idlers are an installed-defective condition that prevents proper belt alignment and increases mechanical stress.",
    "Off-center loading is a non-compliant operating condition contributing to spillage, belt edge damage, and entanglement risk.",
    "Exposed moving belt and rotating components are not adequately guarded per OSHA 1910.212 machine guarding requirements.",
    "Personnel working on the in-service conveyor are exposed to rotating parts and moving belt while diagnosing the fault."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Lock out and tag out the conveyor power source before inspecting or adjusting idlers and loading.",
    "Install or verify fixed guards at all pinch/nip points, rollers, pulleys, and drive components per OSHA 1910.212.",
    "Correct idler alignment and adjust belt tracking per CEMA guidelines.",
    "Center the loading chute/feed point to distribute material evenly across the belt.",
    "Inspect belt edges and splices for damage before returning to service.",
    "Verify emergency pull-cords and start-up alarms are functional before restart."
  ],
  "rationale": "Active belt mistracking from off-center loading and skewed idlers presents a high-severity hazard due to exposed moving/rotating parts, pinch points, and potential for entanglement. The condition is both installed-defective (skewed idlers) and non-compliant (off-center loading/guarding). Work must stop until lockout, guarding, and alignment corrections are completed.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.212 - Machine guarding",
    "CEMA - Belt conveyor design and safety guidelines"
  ]
}
```
